definitions.corpus luteum granulosa lutein layer: A cell layer that forms the inner zone of the corpus luteum, composed of large luteal cells (granulosa lutein cells) derived from luteinized follicular granulosa cells, and that produces oestrogens via aromatase activity using androgens synthesized by the surrounding theca lutein layer.

unresolvable.label: corpus luteum granulosa lutein layer
unresolvable.reason: Input file carried incorrect parent UBERON:0000976 (humerus). Correct parent determined by biological knowledge and OLS4 confirmation: UBERON:0002512 (corpus luteum). No Wikipedia article exists for this term specifically; Wikipedia xref points to the corpus luteum article which describes granulosa lutein cells in detail. SNOMED has equivalent concepts (SNOMED:1351767004 'Structure of granulosa lutein cell layer'; SNOMED:1351869007 'Entire granulosa lutein cell layer') but these are not UBERON IDs and cannot be used as resolved_parents.
unresolvable.suggestion: Add SNOMED cross-references SNOMED:1351767004 and SNOMED:1351869007 manually if curators wish. The genus 'cell layer' is UBERON:0000119. Consider also whether this term is sufficiently distinct from UBERON:0005170 (granulosa cell layer, which is for the pre-ovulatory follicle) to warrant a separate NTR — it is, because the context is the post-ovulatory corpus luteum and the cells are in a luteinized state.
